Lucrarea oferă o privire mai detaliată asupra modului în care boala începe și […] Articolul Inteligența Artificială a dezvăluit schimbări chimice ascunse în creierul afectat de Alzheimer apare prima dată în Descopera .

romania.title: CRPE și CSAPPR semnează un acord de cooperare pentru modernizarea formării profesionale în agricultură, piscicultură și pescuit
romania.summary: București, 5 Martie 2026: Centrul Român de Politici Europene (CRPE) și Comitetul Sectorial Agricultură, Piscicultură și Pescuit din România (CSAPPR) au semnat în luna februarie un Acord de Cooperare pentru… Articolul CRPE și CSAPPR semnează un acord de cooperare pentru modernizarea formării profesionale în agricultură, piscicultură și pescuit apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Este realitatea o iluzie? O nouă teorie sfidează fizica modernă
romania.summary: Un fizician propune că, de fapt, conștiența este baza fundamentală a realității, materia și spațiu-timpul derivând din aceasta. Aceasta este premisa unui nou model teoretic introdus de Maria Stromme, profesor de știința materialelor la Universitatea […] Articolul Este realitatea o iluzie? O nouă teorie sfidează fizica modernă apare prima dată în Descopera .

romania.title: O formă rară de demență l-a făcut pe un bărbat să se îndrăgostească de un sunet
romania.summary: Când auzim cuvântul demență, ne gândim aproape automat la pierderi de memorie. Totuși, deși memoria este frecvent afectată, acesta nu este întotdeauna simptomul dominant. Demența este un termen-umbrelă pentru tulburări cognitive suficient de severe încât […] Articolul O formă rară de demență l-a făcut pe un bărbat să se îndrăgostească de un sunet apare prima dată în Descopera .

romania.title: REVIEW Google Pixel 10a: singurul Android pentru care aș renunța la iPhone
romania.summary: Google Pixel 10a este cel mai nou telefon de la Google și deși nu aduce foarte multe noutăți față de modelul anterior este în continuare un telefon excelent pentru cei care vor o experiență Android...

romania.title: Compania din Târgu Mureș care vrea să cucerească eficiența energetică în Europa Centrală
romania.summary: Proiectele de energie regenerabilă se dezvoltă rapid în România, iar eficiența energetică devine și ea tot mai importantă. În acest context, soluțiile inovatoare de producție și mai ales de stocare...

romania.title: Apple lansează MacBook Air cu cip M5 și 512GB stocare de bază
romania.summary: Apple a anunțat noul MacBook Air echipat cu cipul M5, disponibil în variante de 13 și 15 inch.

romania.title: Bateria cu sodiu care stochează mai multă energie și produce apă potabilă
romania.summary: Cercetători de la Universitatea din Surrey au dezvoltat o baterie sodium-ion care, potrivit rezultatelor publicate, poate stoca aproape de două ori mai multă energie decât variantele convenționale...

romania.title: Rewilding România: obiceiurile umane care rănesc animalele vulnerabile
romania.summary: Rewilding România readuce zimbrul în Munții Țarcu și reconstruiește ecosisteme întregi, dar zimbrii nu sunt singurele specii care au nevoie de ajutor pentru a deveni autosustenabile, nici singurele...

romania.summary: Joi, 12 februarie, de la ora 18:00, la Librăria Libris (Str. Șt. O. Iosif) din Brașov, Rodica Bretin lansează volumul „Pisica de la ora cinci”, apărut în colecția Corint Fiction. … Read More The post Rodica Bretin lansează romanul puzzle „Pisica de la ora cinci” la Librăria Libris din Brașov appeared first on Stiri pozitive .

medical.title: AI model predicts Alzheimer's from MRI brain volume loss with 92.87% accuracy
medical.summary: WPI researchers have used a form of artificial intelligence (AI) to analyze anatomical changes in the brain and predict Alzheimer's disease with nearly 93% accuracy. Their research, published in the journal Neuroscience, also revealed that the anatomical changes, involving loss of brain volume, differ by age and sex.

medical.title: Scientists discover a hidden force that helps wire the brain
medical.summary: Growing neurons rely on chemical cues to find their targets, but new research shows that the brain’s physical properties help shape those signals. Scientists discovered that tissue stiffness can trigger the production of guidance molecules through a force-sensing protein called Piezo1. This protein not only detects mechanical forces but also helps maintain the structure of brain tissue. The discovery reveals a powerful link between the brain’s physical environment and how its wiring is built.

medical.title: Early detection of brain disorders with a single drop of saliva
medical.summary: A team of Korean researchers has, for the first time, developed a technology capable of enabling early diagnosis of major neurological disorders including epilepsy, Parkinson's disease, and schizophrenia using only a small amount of saliva. The study was conducted jointly by a research team led by Dr. Sung-Gyu Park of the Advanced Bio and Healthcare Materials Research Division at the Korea Institute of Materials Science (KIMS), together with Prof. Ho Sang Jung's team at Korea University and researchers from the College of Medicine at The Catholic University of Korea.

medical.title: Review finds exercise could cut cardiometabolic risks in severe mental illness
medical.summary: People with mental illnesses such as schizophrenia, depression or bipolar disorder die on average 10 to 20 years earlier than the general population. The main causes of this are cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, which are triggered or exacerbated by a lack of exercise. Now, an international team of scientists led by MedUni Vienna is calling for physical activity to be recognized as an integral part of psychiatric treatment and is also describing specific steps for successfully integrating it into practice. The review has been published in JAMA Psychiatry.

medical.title: Multi-omics model links medical records to measure biological age
medical.summary: Researchers at the University of Strathclyde have played a central role in an international study that presents a new method to measure biological age—a key factor in predicting health outcomes and understanding the aging process. The study, "OMICmAge quantifies biological age by integrating multi-omics with electronic medical records," led by Harvard University and published in Nature Aging, introduces a new biological aging model developed using a comprehensive range of molecular data drawn from large-scale population studies.

medical.title: AAAAI: No increase seen in asthma exacerbations with ICI use for melanoma, renal cell carcinoma
medical.summary: For patients with asthma, receipt of immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) therapy for melanoma or renal cell carcinoma is not associated with an increased risk for exacerbations, according to a study presented at the annual meeting of the American Academy of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology, held from Feb. 27 to March 2 in Philadelphia.

medical.title: Cannabis compounds show promise in fighting fatty liver disease
medical.summary: Researchers have discovered that non-psychoactive cannabis compounds, CBD and CBG, can significantly reduce liver fat and improve metabolic health. The study reveals that these compounds work by creating a backup energy reserve in the liver and restoring the activity of cellular "cleaning crews" to break down harmful waste. These findings highlight a new, plant-based path for treating the world's most common chronic liver disorder.

science.title: Plant cell structure could hold key to cancer therapies and improved crops
science.summary: Can the bend of a banana give us insight into cancer? What does the shape of a rice grain have to do with infertility? The proteins that give plants their shape and structure are also involved in human disease. A team led by researchers at the University of California, Davis, has mapped out the structure of a key player, augmin, in exhaustive detail. Their work is published in the journal Nature Communications.

science.title: AI-designed diffractive optical processors pave the way for low-power structural health monitoring
science.summary: A team of researchers at the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) has introduced a novel framework for monitoring structural vibrations using diffractive optical processors. This new technology uses artificial intelligence to co-optimize a passive diffractive layer and a shallow neural network, allowing the system to encode time-varying mechanical vibrations into distinct spatiotemporal optical patterns.

science.title: Making mini-lightning in a block of plastic
science.summary: Lightning formation and the conditions triggering it have long been shrouded in a cloud of mystery, but new research led by Penn State scientists is lifting the fog. Using mathematical calculations, the researchers have discovered that lightning-like discharge doesn't require a storm cloud—it could be made inside everyday material on a lab bench. The study is published in the journal Physical Review Letters.

science.title: Researchers create a never-before-seen molecule and prove its exotic nature with quantum computing
science.summary: An international team of scientists from IBM, The University of Manchester, Oxford University, ETH Zurich, EPFL and the University of Regensburg have created and characterized a molecule unlike any previously known—one whose electrons travel through its structure in a corkscrew-like pattern that fundamentally alters its chemical behavior. The work appears in Science.

science.title: NASA now officially has no plans to use new mobile launcher for Artemis
science.summary: When NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced the revamped approach to the Artemis moon program, it was unclear whether the new mobile launcher that has been constructed over the last two years at Kennedy Space Center would ever get used.

science.title: Chemically tuning nanographene into topological spin chains and why the ends matter
science.summary: When most people hear "polymer," they think of plastics. In our group, polymerization is a way to line up identical molecules like beads on a string and let quantum mechanics take over. Put magnetic building blocks in a one-dimensional row and the chain can behave as a single quantum object. Even more intriguing, the chain can hide its most useful properties at its ends.

satire_ro.title: Nominalizarea lui Cerbu la șefia DNA a fost retrasă. Încă e sub contract cu Ciucaș
